scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE Civil Judge (Senior Division)
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute this suit.
2. The Defendant is a person sui juris and can be sued in this Court.
3. The Defendant resides within the jurisdiction of this Court at Koramangala, Bengaluru, and the cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Court.
4. This Court has jurisdiction to try this suit under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.
5. This Court is the Court of the lowest grade competent to try this suit, and the suit value is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

LIMITATION

6. The present suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, being a suit for money lent.

FACTS OF THE TRANSACTION

7. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. 15,00,000 to the Defendant as a hand loan.
8. The amount was transferred via NEFT from the Plaintiff's bank account to the Defendant's account, bearing UTR No. AXIB20240315123456, as evidenced by Annexure A.
9. The Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the said loan amount through WhatsApp messages exchanged between the parties, as evidenced by Annexure B.
10. It was agreed between the parties that the loan amount was to be repaid by 20.06.2024.

DEFAULT AND DEMAND

11. The Defendant failed to repay the loan amount on the agreed date of 20.06.2024.
12. The Plaintiff made repeated oral demands for repayment, but the Defendant did not comply.
13. Consequently, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to the Defendant, as evidenced by Annexure C.
14. Despite receipt of the legal notice, the Defendant has failed to repay the principal amount or respond to the notice.

QUANTIFICATION OF CLAIM

15. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000.
16. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of default i.e., 20.06.2024 till the date of realisation.
17. The interest claimed is simple interest and not compound interest, as per the understanding between the parties.
18. The total claim consists of the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000 along with accrued interest at 12% per annum from 20.06.2024.

LEGAL BASIS

19. The Plaintiff is entitled to the relief sought under Section 37 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, as the Defendant is bound to perform the promise to repay the loan.
20. The bank transaction record bearing UTR No. AXIB20240315123456 proves the advancement of money and establishes consideration.
21. The Defendant's acknowledgment of the loan via WhatsApp messages and the legal notice dated 01.07.2024 establish the subsisting debt.
22. The claimed rate of 12% per annum is within market norms for unsecured lending and not excessive.
23. The Plaintiff has not received any part-payment towards the outstanding amount, and the entire principal remains due.

CAUSE OF ACTION

24. The cause of action first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant failed to repay the loan amount as agreed.
25. The cause of action continues to subsist as the Defendant has not repaid the amount despite demand and legal notice.
26. The suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under the Limitation Act, 1963.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

27. For the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee, the suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only) being the principal claim.
28. The Court fee payable on the plaint is {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} as per {{COURT_FEE_ACT_NAME}}.
29. The plaint is properly stamped and tendered with the requisite court fee.

INTEREST

30. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:
    (a) Pre-suit interest at 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 till the date of institution of the suit;
    (b) Pendente lite interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree, under Order XX Rule 11 CPC;
    (c) Future interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount, under Section 34 CPC.

PRAYER

31. In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:
(a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only);
(b) Award interest on the said amount at the rate of 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 ti
